title: limonium fragile
intro: Limonium fragile, also known as sea lavender, is a flowering plant species that belongs to the Plumbaginaceae family. It is native to the Mediterranean region and is known for its fragrant purple flowers and delicate foliage. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which have been shown to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, fighting off infections, and promoting wound healing. Limonium fragile can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or essential oils, and is often used in aromatherapy to promote relaxation and reduce stress.
